sections.paragraph:
δωρέομαι 1 aor. ἐδωρησάμην; pf. δεδώρημαι, ptc. δεδωρημένος; aor. pass. ptc. neut. δωρηθέν (the act. is scarce in post-Homeric times; the mid. is found Hom.+) to present someth. as a gift or confer a benefit, prob. with some suggestion of formality (cp. Philostrat., Vi. Soph. 2, 10, 7 [589] s.v. δῶρον) present, bestow τινί τι someth. to (on) someone (Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; OGI 90, 31; 517, 7; POxy 1153, 15 [I a.d.]; IDefixWünsch 4, 30; of God Iren. 3, 18, 7 [Harv. II 100, 4]; Hippol., Ref. 9, 30, 4; Did., Gen. 66, 5; of Jesus 81, 25) τὸ πτῶμα τῷ Ἰωσήφ the body to Joseph Mk 15:45. τ. θείας δυνάμεως δεδωρημένης since the divine power has bestowed 2 Pt 1:3. Hence in vs. 4 prob. mid., not pass. (of a divine gift Ael. Aristid. 13 p. 297 D.).—Dg 11:5.—DELG s.v. δίδωμι B. M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

act. act. = active

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

prob. prob. = probable, probably

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

Hippol Hippol , II–III a.d.—List 5

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 266
